CompanyInfo.title: Client
CompanyInfo.details: Escala App - Hospital Israelita Albert Einstein

ProjectInfo.ObjectivesDetails: I joined the Escala Plantões team as a senior developer, where I helped modernize and expand a mission-critical platform used by major hospitals. My goal was to deliver features that made scheduling and management easier for everyone involved.
ProjectInfo.Heading: Challenge
Details: At Escala Plantões, I was deeply involved in maintaining and evolving a large-scale scheduling system trusted by top hospitals. I dove into a legacy codebase, finding ways to improve performance, reliability, and user experience.

I worked on everything from bug fixes to major new features, always with an eye on making the platform more intuitive and powerful for users. Collaborating with product managers, designers, and other developers, I helped turn complex requirements into practical, user-friendly solutions.

I made key decisions about architecture and technology, choosing the right tools to keep the system modern and maintainable. Mentoring junior developers and leading code reviews was a big part of my role—I wanted to help the whole team grow and deliver great work.
Communication was essential. I kept everyone in the loop, making sure stakeholders understood technical trade-offs and the team stayed aligned on goals.

I also focused on documentation and best practices, so future developers could easily pick up where we left off. Staying up-to-date with new tech and encouraging the team to do the same was always a priority.
